description_long: This object is a long, narrow blade with a visibly weathered and corroded surface, suggesting it is made of aged metal, possibly iron or steel. The blade is straight and tapers to a point, with a rectangular cross-section and rough, uneven edges, indicating significant wear or age. There is no visible handle, guard, or decorative elements, and the entire object appears to be a single piece of material. The coloration is dark, with mottled shades of brown and gray, consistent with rust or oxidation. The surface texture is coarse and pitted, lacking any polish or shine. Its utilitarian form and lack of ornamentation suggest it may be an old or primitive knife or sword fragment, possibly archaeological in nature.
description: A long, straight, weathered metal blade with a rough, corroded surface and no visible handle or ornamentation. The object is dark, mottled, and utilitarian in appearance.
description_view_0: The blade appears narrow and tapers to a point, with its rough, corroded surface visible along the length.
description_view_1: This side view shows the full length of the blade, highlighting its straight, rectangular profile and uneven edges.
description_view_2: The object is seen from an angle that emphasizes its thickness and the blunt, squared-off end opposite the point.
description_view_3: A top-down view reveals the flat, elongated shape and the coarse, pitted texture along the blade's surface.
attribute_handle_design: none visible; blade extends the full length with no distinct handle
